old_claim.note: Leather Tanning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No leather-tanning dependency remains because the basic early bearing is the rotating wooden axle supported in a chassis groove; leather liners are optional refinements.

source_shape.source_locator: Early-vehicle discussion explains that fixed wheels and their axle rotated together in transverse chassis grooves, which themselves constituted the bearing.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The earliest plain-bearing architecture can be formed entirely by the wooden axle and its supporting chassis surface.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Leather can reduce wear in some designs but its absence does not break the fundamental bearing mechanism represented by this node.

ontology_before: The generic early cart bearing required one optional lining material.
ontology_after: The Ljubljana rotating-axle system is modeled from its wooden wheel, axle, and chassis support.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: cart_axle_bearings no longer directly depends on leather_tanning.
would_reject_if: Analysis of the Ljubljana assembly established that a leather liner was necessary for its axle support to function.
short_reason: The plain bearing works as a wooden axle in a chassis groove.
